seoTitle: Mount Hicks vs Mount Nelson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Hicks and Mount Nelson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Hicks vs Mount Nelson: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Hicks and Mount Nelson.
heading: Mount Hicks vs Mount Nelson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Hicks, Tasmania and Mount Nelson, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Nelson has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Mount Hicks (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Hicks or Mount Nelson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Hicks has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Mount Nelson scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Hicks scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Nelson scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Hicks or Mount Nelson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Nelson has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
